title: Snap Q3 2023 Earnings Report
text:
Snap Q3 2023 Earnings Report

Financial Performance

Snap exceeded Wall Street expectations in Q3 2023, posting $1.19 billion in revenue, a 5% year-over-year increase. The company reported a net loss of $368 million, or 23 cents per share, better than analyst consensus estimates of $1.11 billion revenue and a 24-cent loss.

CEO Evan Spiegel noted that Our revenue returned to positive growth in Q3, increasing 5% year-over-year and emphasized efforts to improve advertising effectiveness.

User Metrics

Snapchat's daily active user base grew to 406 million, up 9 million users from the previous period and matching Wall Street projections of 405.8 million.

Additional user engagement highlights include:
- Snapchat+ subscribers: Exceeded 5 million at $4/month, suggesting a $240+ million annual run rate
- My AI chatbot usage: Over 200 million people have exchanged 20+ billion messages since April 2023 launch

Q4 Outlook & Challenges

Management provided conservative guidance of $1.32-$1.375 billion for Q4, representing 2-6% growth. The company cited limited visibility into advertising demand, partly attributing uncertainty to the Israel-Hamas conflict, which prompted major brands to pause spending.

Organizational Changes

COO Jerry Hunter announced his retirement after seven years, with a planned departure date of July 1, 2024.
